user_prompt: Explain Bernoulli’s principle using an airplane wing in depth. Use a single commercial airplane wing flying steadily through the air at cruising speed. No humans. Scene 1: establish the wing and airflow, showing smooth air approaching and splitting above and below the wing. Scene 2: zoom closer to the wing cross-section and clearly show faster airflow over the curved upper surface and slower airflow below, with visualized streamlines and pressure difference (without text). Scene 3: show the resulting lift force acting upward on the wing, with subtle arrows or particle motion (no labels, no text), and the airplane continuing stable flight. Cinematic realism, physically accurate airflow, smooth continuous camera motion, consistent lighting and environment, no jump cuts. No text, no logos, no symbols, no humans.

style_bible.visual_style: Cinematic realism with physically accurate airflow visualization, smooth particle effects for air movement, subtle color grading emphasizing natural daylight and atmospheric depth.
style_bible.camera_rules: Continuous smooth camera movements without jump cuts; maintain consistent perspective on the airplane wing; no sudden zooms or cuts; camera always outside the aircraft showing airflow clearly.
style_bible.lighting_rules: Soft natural daylight from a consistent direction, mimicking mid-morning sun; balanced highlights and shadows to emphasize wing curvature and airflow; no harsh contrasts or artificial lighting.
style_bible.continuity_rules: Primary subject (airplane wing) and environment (clear blue sky with distant clouds) remain consistent across scenes; airflow visualization style consistent; camera movement flows naturally from scene to scene.

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Establish the airplane wing in steady flight and visualize smooth airflow splitting above and below it.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY (state carried across scenes):
- primary subject: commercial airplane wing
- environment: clear blue sky with soft distant clouds
- explanatory state:
  - Scene 1: baseline condition (observation)

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: wide shot with wing centered horizontally, showing full wing span from root to tip
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly forward alongside wing leading edge
- subject & action: wing steadily moving left to right; smooth, semi-transparent airflow lines approach wing from left, splitting smoothly above and below
- environment details: blue sky background, soft white clouds far away, subtle horizon line
- lighting: soft sunlight from upper left, natural daylight color temperature
- cinematic look: natural film stock vibe with moderate contrast and shallow depth of field on wing edges

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: medium shot focusing on mid-wing section
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow pan following airflow direction
- subject & action: airflow lines continue smoothly, clearly splitting at leading edge, flowing over and under wing surfaces
- environment details: sky and clouds remain consistent, slight motion blur on distant clouds
- lighting: consistent soft sunlight, gentle shadows on wing curvature
- cinematic look: realistic texture detail on wing surface, smooth airflow visualization

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: medium close-up on wing cross-section profile from side
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow tilt downward following airflow path
- subject & action: airflow lines clearly visible approaching and dividing, no turbulence yet
- environment details: sky background with faint cloud wisps
- lighting: soft directional light highlighting wing curvature
- cinematic look: crisp focus on wing profile and airflow

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.camera: slow dolly forward alongside wing leading edge, then slow pan following airflow, ending with slow tilt downward on wing cross-section
scenes.audio_cue: Here we see a commercial airplane wing cruising steadily through clear skies. Notice how the air approaches the wing and splits smoothly above and below its surface, setting the stage for the forces that keep the plane aloft.

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Reveal the cause of lift by showing faster airflow over the curved upper wing surface and slower airflow below, with visualized streamlines and pressure differences.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY (state carried across scenes):
- primary subject: commercial airplane wing
- environment: clear blue sky with soft distant clouds
- explanatory state:
  - Scene 2: mechanism revealed (cause)

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: close-up on wing cross-section profile from side
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow dolly in towards upper wing surface
- subject & action: detailed airflow streamlines appear; lines above wing become visibly faster and more closely spaced, lines below are slower and more spaced
- environment details: blurred sky background, subtle cloud shapes
- lighting: soft directional light emphasizing wing curvature
- cinematic look: high detail on airflow speed differences, subtle glow on fast streamlines

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: extreme close-up on upper and lower wing surfaces
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow pan along wing surface from leading to trailing edge
- subject & action: animated pressure gradient visualized by subtle color shading on wing surface—cooler blue hues above indicating lower pressure, warmer tones below indicating higher pressure; airflow speed differences remain clear
- environment details: minimal background, focus on wing and airflow
- lighting: consistent soft sunlight
- cinematic look: realistic texture on wing with smooth gradient shading

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: medium close-up on wing cross-section with full airflow
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow tilt up revealing full airflow pattern
- subject & action: streamlines show faster flow over top, slower below; pressure difference visually emphasized by gentle particle motion pushing upward
- environment details: sky background with distant clouds
- lighting: natural daylight
- cinematic look: smooth, continuous airflow visualization with clear cause-effect cues

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.camera: slow dolly in to upper wing surface close-up, then slow pan along wing surface, ending with slow tilt up showing full airflow pattern
scenes.audio_cue: The wing's curved upper surface causes air to speed up, creating lower pressure above, while slower air below maintains higher pressure. This pressure difference is the key mechanism generating lift.

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Show the resulting lift force acting upward on the wing and the airplane continuing stable flight.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY (state carried across scenes):
- primary subject: commercial airplane wing
- environment: clear blue sky with soft distant clouds
- explanatory state:
  - Scene 3: outcome explained (effect)

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: medium shot of wing from below and side, showing underside and top curvature
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow crane upward and slight forward tracking
- subject & action: subtle upward arrows and particle flow illustrate lift force pushing wing upward; airplane wing remains steady
- environment details: blue sky with soft clouds
- lighting: soft sunlight highlighting wing underside and top
- cinematic look: realistic textures and smooth particle effects

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: wide shot showing full wing and part of fuselage from side
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly backward tracking airplane moving steadily forward
- subject & action: lift force arrows remain visible; airplane maintains steady altitude and speed
- environment details: sky and horizon line with distant clouds
- lighting: consistent daylight
- cinematic look: natural film stock with balanced contrast

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: wide shot of airplane wing in flight with smooth airflow continuing
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow pan following airplane wing forward
- subject & action: lift force arrows fade gently as airplane continues stable flight; airflow remains smooth
- environment details: clear sky with soft clouds
- lighting: natural soft daylight
- cinematic look: cinematic realism with depth of field on wing

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.camera: slow crane upward and forward tracking wing, then slow dolly backward tracking airplane, ending with slow pan following wing forward
scenes.audio_cue: This pressure difference creates an upward lift force on the wing, allowing the airplane to maintain stable flight as it moves steadily through the air.
